title: cota tinctoria subsp. fussii
intro: Cota tinctoria subsp. fussii, also known as Dyer's Chamomile or Cota, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The health benefits of Cota tinctoria subsp. fussii include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as acting as a natural remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Cota tinctoria subsp. fussii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or topical cream, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy for various health issues.
